title: Settings, Keybinds & Accessibility

use_cases.name: Open Settings Menu
use_cases.description: Player opens main settings menu which shows categories: Game, Graphics, Sound, Language, Controls (keyboard only), Data, and any custom mod categories.

use_cases.name: Adjust Game Settings
use_cases.description: Player toggles/sliders for: save interval, autotarget (mobile), keyboard mode, community servers, save on create, block replace, conveyor pathfinding, hints, background pause, build auto-pause, double-tap mine, command mode hold, mod crash disable, player limit (Steam), Steam public host, console.

use_cases.name: Adjust Graphics Settings
use_cases.description: Player configures: UI edge padding, UI scale, screen shake, bloom intensity/blur, FPS cap, chat opacity, laser/unit/bridge opacity, zoom multipliers, vsync, fullscreen, landscape (Android), effects, atmosphere, lighting, minimap, smooth camera, position display, FPS display, player indicators, pings, build plans, weather, animated water/shields, bloom, pixelate, linear filtering, skip core animation, hide displays, Mac notch.

use_cases.name: Adjust Sound Settings
use_cases.description: Player sets: always play music, music volume (0-100), SFX volume (0-100), ambient volume (0-100).

use_cases.name: Change Language
use_cases.description: Player selects from 35+ locales. Saves locale string to Core.settings, sets player.locale, shows restart prompt. Auto-detects closest locale on first run via findClosestLocale().

use_cases.name: Remap Keybinds
use_cases.description: Player searches keybinds by name, views all KeyBind entries grouped by category, clicks Rebind to open capture dialog (openDialog()), presses key/mouse/scroll to assign. Can reset individual key or all keys via KeyBind.resetAll().
flow: rebuildBinds() — populates list from KeyBind.all filtered by searchText
openDialog(keybind) — shows modal Dialog with InputListener
rebind(bind, newKey) — assigns new Axis/key value, calls bind.save()
For axis binds: two-pass rebind (min key first, then max key)

use_cases.name: View / Select Visual Effects
use_cases.description: Player browses animated effect previews (from LogicFx.entries() or all Fx fields). Each EffectCell renders live animation. Player can select an effect (listener callback) or just browse. Factory method withAllEffects() exposes all Fx class fields.
use_cases.show_with_callback: show(Cons<EffectEntry> listener) — returns selected effect to caller

use_cases.name: View Discord Integration
use_cases.description: Player views Discord invite prompt. Can copy Discord URL to clipboard or open it in browser. Falls back to clipboard copy if URI open fails.

use_cases.name: Manage Data
use_cases.description: Player can: clear all data (keeps UUID/USID), clear saves, clear research/unlocks, clear campaign saves, export data as ZIP, import data from ZIP (restarts app), open data folder (desktop), export crash logs.
sub_actions: clearresearch → universe.clearLoadoutInfo() + TechTree.all reset + content unlocks cleared
clearcampaignsaves → planet/sector stats cleared, sector saves deleted
export → exportData(file) → ZIP of settings, maps, saves, mods, schematics
import → importData(file) → unzip to data dir + app.exit()
crash export → getLogs() writes crashes + last_log.txt

use_cases.name: Add Custom Settings Category (Mod API)
use_cases.description: Mods call addCategory(name, icon, builder) to inject custom SettingsTable into the settings menu. Custom categories appear in rebuildMenu() and visible() routing.
use_cases.method: addCategory(String name, Drawable icon, Cons<SettingsTable> builder)

use_cases.name: Reset Settings to Defaults
use_cases.description: Each SettingsTable has a 'Reset to Defaults' button that calls settings.remove(name) for all entries and rebuilds the table.
use_cases.trigger: SettingsTable.rebuild() button → settings.remove(setting.name) for each Setting

key_relationships: SettingsMenuDialog extends BaseDialog; contains SettingsTable instances (game, graphics, sound)
SettingsMenuDialog navigates to LanguageDialog (ui.language.show()) and KeybindDialog (ui.controls.show())
SettingsMenuDialog contains internal dataDialog (BaseDialog) for data management
KeybindDialog extends Dialog; creates ephemeral rebindDialog (Dialog) per rebind action
EffectsDialog extends BaseDialog; contains inner EffectCell (Element) and BoundsBatch (Batch)
SettingsTable extends Table; contains Seq<Setting>; Setting subtypes: CheckSetting, SliderSetting, TextSetting, AreaTextSetting
SettingsCategory (inner class of SettingsMenuDialog) wraps name + icon + SettingsTable for mod extensions
include_extend_notes: UC05 (Language), UC06 (Keybinds) are accessed via UC01 (Settings Menu) — «extend» from Player via menu buttons
UC09 (Data) is a sub-dialog launched from UC01 — «include»
UC10 (Mod Category) «extends» UC01 at the rebuildMenu() extension point
UC07 (Effects) can be standalone or called with a listener for effect selection from logic editor
important_notes: SettingsMenuDialog uses a back-stack pattern: rebuildMenu() shows the category list; visible(index) swaps in a specific SettingsTable. All settings persisted via Core.settings (key-value). Platform-conditional settings exist for mobile/iOS/desktop/Steam/Mac. KeybindDialog supports two-pass axis rebinding (min key then max key).
